title: IDE-IPA-Railway: ALIVE Framework V2.0 MCP Server for Innovation-Driven Enterprise Assessment
description:
An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server implementing the ALIVE Framework V2.0 (Agentic Longitudinal IDE-IPA Value Evaluation) for evaluating Innovation-Driven Enterprises (IDEs) under Thailand's PMUC funding scheme.

Architecture:

  • 100-point rubric scoring across four parts: Part A (Standard IDE Assessment, 28 points), Part B (Research-Specific Assessment, 22 points), Part C (Impact Pathway Logic / Ex-Ante Analysis, 25 points), Part D (SROI Assessment, 25 points)
  • PACT (Plan-Act-Check-Track) longitudinal coaching loop with auto-calibrating halting
  • Dual-database architecture: PostgreSQL for assessment history and assessor records; ChromaDB for semantic memory using paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2 (384-dim, Thai-English bilingual)
  • L6 Knowledge Broker tools for cross-agent semantic learning, peer matching, and lesson-atom extraction
  • Self-evaluation loop with task-specific keyword matching
  • Production deployment on Railway with FastMCP transport

Version 2.1.2 changes: Idempotent l6_kb_extract_lessons tool generates deterministic MD5-hash atom_ids instead of timestamp-based IDs, eliminating duplicate atoms in shared_kb on retry.

Reproducibility: This Zenodo archive captures the exact codebase used to generate empirical results reported in the associated ALIVE Framework academic paper.

creators.name: Numsomran, Arjin
creators.affiliation: Department of Instrumentation and Control Engineering, School of Engineering, King Mongkut Institute of Technology Ladkrabang (KMITL)

notes: Developed at the Department of Instrumentation and Control Engineering, KMITL. This software supports academic research on AI-driven longitudinal evaluation of Innovation-Driven Enterprises under Thailand PMUC funding scheme.
